title: Dry Bulk Orderbook Accelerates as Greek Owners Lead Newbuilding Momentum
section_path: Dry Bulk Orderbook Accelerates as Greek Owners Lead Newbuilding Momentum
section_path_text: Dry Bulk Orderbook Accelerates as Greek Owners Lead Newbuilding Momentum
summary:
Main
Image reference: assets/2026-07-07_dry-bulk-orderbook-accelerates-as-greek-owners-lead-newbuilding-momentum_img_unsplash-image-b-8enpognaw_d90d258514f5.jpg
By Eirini Diamantara & Dimitris Roumeliotis
The dry bulk orderbook has expanded meaningfully during the first half of 2026, pointing to a market that continues to invest in fleet renewal despite the uncertainty surrounding future fuels, shipyard pricing and long-term demand visibility. As of Jan-Jun 2026, the bulk carrier orderbook stands at 1,642 vessels against an active fleet of 14,908 units, translating into an orderbook-to-fleet ratio of 11.0%. This compares with 1,363 vessels on order against a fleet of 14,360 units during the same period of 2025, when the ratio stood at 9.5%.
